title: Ritual Dance Choreographer
description: The user choreographs a synchronized ritual dance between a ReadyPlayer.me avatar and the goddess Nemetona inside a sacred grove. Players sequence animation clips, adjust timing offsets, mirror movements, and manage an energy conservation system where total kinetic energy across both characters must remain constant. Full undo/redo support lets users refine the choreography.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads both GLB models: shared_assets/readyplayer.me.glb (humanoid avatar) and shared_assets/nemetona.glb (goddess figure). Place them facing each other 3 units apart on a circular stone platform. Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0,2,6) with OrbitControls. Add ambient light (0.4), a warm directional light from above-right, and a subtle green point light between the characters for atmosphere.

Implement a choreography timeline system as an HTML overlay panel at the bottom. Each character has a track showing animation clip blocks. Enumerate all AnimationClips from both GLBs and list them in a draggable palette. Users drag clips onto either character's timeline track (steps 1-4). Each clip block can be click-selected and repositioned along the timeline with arrow keys to adjust start-time offsets in 0.25s increments (steps 5-6). A mirror toggle button copies the selected clip to the other character with X-axis mirrored root motion (steps 7-8).

Implement an energy conservation law: define a total energy budget of 100 units. Each animation clip has an energy cost proportional to its duration multiplied by a speed multiplier slider (0.5x-2.0x). Display energy bars for each character and a shared total. If adding or speeding up a clip would exceed 100 total energy, automatically reduce the other character's newest clip speed to compensate, visually indicated by a yellow warning flash. Users can manually redistribute by adjusting speed sliders (steps 9-10).

Provide undo/redo buttons (and Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Shift+Z) that track all operations: clip placement, removal, repositioning, mirroring, and speed changes, stored in a command stack. A Play button runs the choreography using THREE.AnimationMixer for each model, blending overlapping clips. Show a playhead scrubber on the timeline. Use THREE.Clock for timing. Style the UI with semi-transparent dark panels and white text.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  avatarClipCount: number,         // total animation clips loaded from readyplayer.me.glb
  goddessClipCount: number,         // total animation clips loaded from nemetona.glb
  avatarTimelineSlots: number,         // count of clips placed on avatar timeline track
  goddessTimelineSlots: number,         // count of clips placed on goddess timeline track
  selectedClipIndex: number,         // index of currently selected timeline clip block (-1 if none)
  totalEnergyBudget: number,         // maximum allowed energy units (fixed at 100)
  currentEnergyUsed: number,         // sum of energy costs from all placed clips
  mirrorModeActive: boolean,         // whether mirror toggle is currently enabled
  timelinePlayheadPosition: number,         // current playback time in seconds
  isSynchronizedPlaying: boolean,         // whether both characters are animating in sync
  lastOffsetAdjustmentTime: number,         // timestamp of last arrow key offset adjustment in ms
  energyViolationDetected: boolean,         // true if currentEnergyUsed exceeds totalEnergyBudget
}
